keyword: best pet camera for multiple cats
title: Best Pet Camera for Multiple Cats (Whole-Room Coverage)
meta: Cameras that cover a multi-cat home — wide angle, pan-tilt, and multi-pet tracking.
intro: With several cats, you need to see the whole room, not one corner. Wide field of view and pan-tilt beat a fixed narrow lens.

sections.body: Aim for 140°+ or a pan-tilt model so one camera covers a large room.
Some apps let you tag pets; most just flag motion. Don't overpay for 'AI pet recognition' that mislabels.
Ceiling corner mount sees more than a low shelf when cats use vertical space.

products.name: 360 Pan-Tilt Pet Camera
products.asin_query: pan tilt pet camera 360 wide angle

faq.q: Can one camera watch two rooms?
faq.a: No — you need one per room or a wide common area.
Motion alerts fire on any movement; pet-specific detection reduces but doesn't eliminate them.
